key_points: Digital-only IDs cause real access problems when phones die
Security incidents on campus including fire extinguisher attack in dorms
Rose Hill dorms described positively; mattresses unexpectedly beloved
key_quotes: Way too much reliance on phones. You're in Manhattan all day and your phone is dead and now you do not have an ID.
Two years ago a mentally deranged man broke through the unguarded turnstile gate and sprayed multiple residents with a fire extinguisher in their university apartment.
Never slept better than on the dorm mattresses.

key_points: Social scene described as low-energy; students called 'lames' by observers
Making friends requires real effort; isolation is a genuine freshman risk
Rose Hill has traditional campus energy; LC feels more 'office-like'
key_quotes: Juice WRLD and Charli XCX did a free performance at Fordham in collaboration with Tinder, and only 200 people showed up; sounds like a bunch of lames to me.
LC just felt office-like to me vs a college.
I just graduated in May. If you grew up humble and are from a smaller town/area...Fordham is not the place. I found it very hard to make friends.

key_points: Students wish the Walsh Library allowed eating inside
Food complaints appear among top student grievances
Arthur Avenue nearby praised as a genuine dining perk
key_quotes: They should invent a Walsh Library that lets you eat inside.
The Italian restaurants on Arthur Avenue are some of the best ever!
For a university that charges over 50k in tuition and fees I'm shocked that these are issues they're still having.

key_points: Freshman isolation and depression reported as a real pattern
Intense workload means missing even one class causes serious catch-up stress
MSW grad students report 'not having a quality life' during the program
key_quotes: In my dorm pondering like did I make the right decision? Why am I depressed, why did I pick a school where I was gonna be so dependent on making friends.
I literally did not have a quality life like a decent human being while doing MSW program at Fordham.
Fordham courses have an intense workload and even missing one day can make it hard to catch up.

key_points: Financial aid packages routinely shock students; one got $17k on a $50k income family
Net cost of $47k–$51k/year reported even after aid and scholarships
Financial aid office slow to respond; appeals process opaque for first-gen students
key_quotes: Got my financial aid package. I'd need to pay 51,000 PER YEAR. Wtf. Who is affording ts.
Negative SAI and got the deans scholarship yet I only got 17k of need-based aid and my net direct charges are 47k. My parent makes 50k a year with 4 dependents.
I tried talking to financial aid, but there wasn't much they could do. Also, the CSS Profile asks for wayyyy too much info.

key_points: Workload is genuinely heavy; grade deflation noted especially for STEM
Small class sizes (~20 students) praised as a real academic advantage
Comp sci department specifically called out as 'not worth it'
key_quotes: Can you comment on the grade deflation for STEM majors.
The comp sci department is not worth it.
Their classes are really small, like 20-students per class...organized in like a U with three long tables encircling the professor. It seems like a really nice place to learn.

key_points: President Tetlow is deeply unpopular; students call for her removal
Administration seen as protecting reputation over students in disputes
Tuition raised 4–5% annually while spending on rebrand angered students
key_quotes: Fordham seems more focused on protecting its reputation than on supporting its students, especially when there's clear wrongdoing.
They raise tuition every single year by 4-5%, then turn around and spend thousands to change the logo.
If Tetlow had any respect for the Alumni and actually cared to listen to the people who pay her salary, she could and would 100% do this.

key_points: Rose Hill is genuinely beautiful; Gothic architecture praised repeatedly
Campus is safe; immediately outside campus requires awareness
LC is urban and convenient; Rose Hill offers traditional college feel
key_quotes: Campus is very safe. Outside of campus is not as safe.
Whatever its good and bad points, it LOOKS like college.
If you dont want to spend 10 dollars to go to manhattan, then take the D train downtown...It's $2.90 to go and $2.90 coming back.

key_points: NYC location enables internships during the school year — real advantage
Finance program ranked #15 nationally; strong Wall Street placement cited
Alumni network described as genuinely active and helpful
key_quotes: Being in NYC means you can intern during the school year, build real relationships, and stack experience before recruiting even ramps up.
Fordham's finance program is highly regarded and places well into top firms, largely because students have consistent access to opportunities and a strong alumni network.
The alumni network is strong and the way internships are marketed here — are they as big of a deal as people say?

key_points: Widely considered overpriced; $94k/year total cost cited as dealbreaker
Strong merit scholarships exist but need-based aid underdelivers
NYC location and finance outcomes partially justify cost for business students
key_quotes: Fordham isn't worth 94k a year.
I went to Fordham in the late 90s. Had a great experience, but don't run up a lot of debt to do it!
It's fair to judge the cost of the school and its value...I am an alum, and really sickened by how much it costs.

key_points: Wealthier, preppy student body; class diversity is a real gap
Rose Hill has strong community feel; LC is more transient and urban
Students who find their people love it; those who don't are miserable
key_quotes: I was jarred out of my reverie when I found the people to be rich, bobble-headed, self-indulgent social climbers whose only life's priorities is to club, party, etc.
Once you find your people it gets easier to be more involved.
Diversity can't happen as long as the tuition is high. Minorities can't afford to come here and the worst part is the Bronx is a minority region!!

key_points: **Financial Aid**: Packages shock low-income students; $51k/year net cost reported
**Administration**: President deeply unpopular; tuition hikes fund rebrands not students
**Security**: Dorm intruder incidents reported; digital ID system creates real access gaps
key_quotes: My time at Fordham taught me a tough lesson: it often feels like no one is truly looking out for you.
A mentally deranged man broke through the unguarded turnstile gate and sprayed multiple residents with a fire extinguisher.
They raise tuition every single year by 4-5%, then turn around and spend thousands to change the logo.

key_points: Arthur Avenue is one of NYC's best restaurant streets — steps from Rose Hill
ROTC program at Fordham's NYC unit described as 'amazing'
POTS food pantry volunteer opportunity is easy, resume-worthy, 7 min from campus
key_quotes: The Italian restaurants on Arthur Avenue are some of the best ever! And their career services program and internship network are great.
The NROTC unit in NYC is amazing!
I volunteered with POTS (part of the solution). It's about a 7 min walk from campus and it's a great resume booster for ~4hr of easy work a week.
